claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 17 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 17 and not in approved education or extension period
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child works 24+ hours/week and is not in approved education
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Child is 17 and not in approved education or extension period; Child works 24+ hours/week and is not in approved education
circumstances: Child is 17 and not in approved education or extension period
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child works 24+ hours/week and is not in approved education
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me start by gathering some information about your situation.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
To assess your eligibility for Child Benefit, I need to ask you a few questions. Remember, Child Benefit is assessed for each child individually.

First question: **Do you have a child or children that you're responsible for?** If yes, could you tell me how many children you're inquiring about?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have one child. I'm responsible for my son Alex, who is 17 years old.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. You have one child, Alex, who is 17 years old.

Since Alex is 17 (which falls in the 16-19 age range), I need to check if he meets the education or training requirements for Child Benefit eligibility.

**Is Alex currently in approved education or training?** 

This would include:
- Full-time non-advanced education (such as A-levels, Scottish Highers, or equivalent)
- Approved training programs

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex isn't in any education or training at the moment. He finished school but isn't doing A-levels or anything like that.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
I understand. Since Alex is not currently in education or training, I need to check one more thing:

**Did Alex recently leave education or approved training?** Specifically, has it been within the last 20 weeks since he stopped?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, it's been longer than that. He left school a while ago now.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing that information. Based on what you've told me, I can now make a determination about your Child Benefit eligibility for Alex.

**Eligibility Decision:**

Unfortunately, you are **not eligible** for Child Benefit for Alex at this time.

**Reason:**

For children aged 16-19, Child Benefit can only continue if they are:
- In approved full-time non-advanced education (such as A-levels, Scottish Highers, or equivalent), OR
- In approved training, OR
- Within 20 weeks of leaving education/training (in which case there's a limited extension period)

Since Alex is 17, not currently in education or training, and left school more than 20 weeks ago, he does not meet the qualifying child criteria for Child Benefit.

**Important to note:**

If Alex decides to return to approved education or training in the future (before he turns 20), you may become eligible for Child Benefit again during that period. You would need to notify HMRC if his circumstances change.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit eligibility?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I understand now. Thank you for explaining that to me. That's disappointing but it makes sense - since Alex isn't studying or in training and it's been a while since he left school, he doesn't qualify. I appreciate you taking the time to go through this with me.
